headline: Alphabet’s Mapping Breakthrough And Clean Energy Push Reshape Growth Prospects
summary: Google, part of Alphabet (NasdaqGS:GOOGL), receives conditional approval from South Korea to export high precision map data under strict security rules. The decision clears a long running data sovereignty hurdle and allows a more complete Google Maps service in the Korean market. Alphabet also announces new clean powered data centers in Minnesota and Texas featuring what it describes as the world’s largest long duration battery system. The company pairs these sites with energy partnerships...

headline: Qualcomm and Other Industry Leaders Commit to 6G Trajectory Towards Commercialization Starting from 2029 Onwards
summary: BARCELONA, Spain, March 02, 2026--Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: QCOM) today announced a new strategic coalition with leading industry partners to accelerate the development and global deployment of 6G. Revealed at MWC Barcelona 2026, the collaboration establishes a clear, milestone-driven roadmap focused on delivering 6G commercial systems starting from 2029 onwards.

content.title: Qualcomm and Other Industry Leaders Commit to 6G Trajectory Towards Commercialization Starting from 2029 Onwards
content.summary: BARCELONA, Spain, March 02, 2026--Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: QCOM) today announced a new strategic coalition with leading industry partners to accelerate the development and global deployment of 6G. Revealed at MWC Barcelona 2026, the collaboration establishes a clear, milestone-driven roadmap focused on delivering 6G commercial systems starting from 2029 onwards.

headline: $81.04 Bn Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) Markets - Global Forecast to 2030 with IBM, Cisco, AWS, Google, Microsoft, Oracle, HPE, Intel, Hitachi, and SAP Leading
summary: The global AIoT market is projected to grow from USD 25.44 billion in 2025 to USD 81.04 billion by 2030, driven by the integration of AI and IoT, enhancing real-time analytics. Key growth factors include 5G rollout, edge computing advancements, and low-cost sensors. Smart city initiatives and Industry 4.0 are accelerating demand, though challenges like data security and implementation costs persist. Asia-Pacific is expected to lead in growth. Major players include IBM, Cisco, Google, and Microso

headline: Meta Resets AI Hardware Plan With AMD And Google Partnerships
summary: Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) has halted its in-house Olympus AI accelerator project after running into technical and manufacturing hurdles. The company has signed multi billion dollar agreements with AMD and Google to secure large volumes of AI chips and cloud compute capacity. Meta is considering taking a stake of up to 10% in AMD as part of a broader shift toward a diversified AI hardware supply chain. These moves are set to influence how Meta builds and delivers AI powered products...

content.title: Meta Resets AI Hardware Plan With AMD And Google Partnerships
content.summary: Meta Platforms (NasdaqGS:META) has halted its in-house Olympus AI accelerator project after running into technical and manufacturing hurdles. The company has signed multi billion dollar agreements with AMD and Google to secure large volumes of AI chips and cloud compute capacity. Meta is considering taking a stake of up to 10% in AMD as part of a broader shift toward a diversified AI hardware supply chain. These moves are set to influence how Meta builds and delivers AI powered products...
